title: ErrorBoundary missing for detail panels (crash risk)
body:
Several detail panels (NewsPanel, AnalystPanel subcomponents) are not wrapped with ErrorBoundary. If any panel component throws during render, the entire detail view crashes without recovery UI. ErrorBoundary.tsx (line 13-54) provides crash recovery, but is only applied at module level, not at individual panel level.

Currently exposed: NewsPanel (src/renderer/src/modules/finance/detail/panels/NewsPanel.tsx:3), AnalystPanel (line 26), FinancialRatios (line 9), FinancialsCharts (line 14). If any throws, user sees blank white screen with no error message or retry button.

Resilience impact: Panel crashes cascade to entire detail page, blocking user from accessing other tabs or data. User must reload app or navigate away.

Proposed: Wrap high-risk panels (those fetching external data, rendering dynamic charts) with ErrorBoundary and a fallback UI that surfaces the panel's error without killing siblings.

Severity: P1 - data-critical feature (stock detail) becomes inaccessible on any panel error.
evidence: src/renderer/src/modules/finance/detail/panels/NewsPanel.tsx:3-18 (no error boundary), src/renderer/src/modules/finance/detail/panels/AnalystPanel.tsx:26-450 (complex render logic, no boundary)

title: Yahoo auth crumb expiry (401 response) user-visible failure
body:
YahooAuth.ts implements crumb caching (30-minute TTL) and 401 retry logic (line 78-91), but user never sees the auth failure message. When crumb expires mid-session and a 401 occurs:

1. yahooFetch forces fresh crumb (line 80: getYahooAuth(true))
2. Retry succeeds or fails silently
3. If retry fails, response.ok is false but component just renders empty/null state
4. User has no idea why data disappeared

Currently: catch blocks in service layer (line 80) swallow errors. Upstream UI (e.g., FinancialsCharts line 28-34) shows generic "Loading financial charts…" with no error indication.

Proposed: Propagate auth errors to UI layer (return error in query.error instead of silent retry). Display user-visible message like "Session expired—refresh required" with retry button.

Resilient apps: Degrade gracefully, inform user why data stopped arriving.

title: Inconsistent loading UX across finance panels (spinner vs text vs none)
body:
Loading states are inconsistently handled across detail panels:

1. FinancialRatios.tsx:105-110 - Plain text: "Loading ratios…" (no spinner, blocks interaction)
2. FinancialsCharts.tsx:28-34 - Plain text: "Loading financial charts…" (no visual feedback)
3. AnalystPanel.tsx:191-204 - Two states: verbose text "Loading statements + fundamentals…" then "Insufficient historical data…"
4. SearchBar.tsx:27 - No loading UI at all (results appear instantly from cache or hang silently)
5. TopMovers.tsx:9-92 - No loading state (component renders immediately with empty data)

Issue: Users see different UX per panel. No skeleton loaders, spinners, or progress indicators. Panel rendering feels slow or broken.

Proposed: Standardize on skeleton loader component for financial data panels. Use consistent pattern: isLoading ? <Skeleton /> : <Content />.

Resilient apps: Provide immediate visual feedback that async work is in flight, not silence.

title: Position/quote array index mapping assumes parallel order (race condition)
body:
PositionsList.tsx (line 15-23) maps positions to quotes using array index:

```typescript
const enriched = positions.map((p, i) => {
  const q = quotes[i]?.data  // ASSUMES quotes[i] matches positions[i]
  ...
})
```

This breaks if:
1. Quotes arrive out-of-order from react-query (network delay per ticker)
2. One quote request fails while others succeed (sparse array)
3. Positions array reorders but quotes don't

Example: positions = [AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL], quotes network returns [MSFT, null, GOOGL] → AAPL gets MSFT price, MSFT gets GOOGL price.

Proposed: Use object lookup: const quoteMap = quotes.reduce((m, q) => { m[q.queryKey[1]] = q; return m; }, {}); then enrich with quoteMap[p.ticker].data.

Severity: P2 - silently shows wrong prices in portfolio (financial accuracy bug).

title: Missing network offline detection (infinite loading)
body:
App has no offline detection or offline-aware fallback. When network drops:

1. All queries (quote, statements, fundamentals) hang indefinitely
2. Loading states never resolve ("Loading ratios…" persists)
3. User sees frozen UI, no indication that network is down
4. No offline banner or "retry" button

React-query is configured without networkMode or retry policy (useQuote, useStatements, useFundamentals all use default settings). Queries retry forever on network failure without backoff.

Proposed: 
1. Add navigator.onOnline listener to global context
2. Disable queries when offline, show offline banner
3. Set explicit retry/staleTime: { retry: 1, retryDelay: 1000, networkMode: 'online' }
4. Provide "Retry" button when offline recovers

Resilient apps: Degrade gracefully, inform user of network state.

title: Empty state copy lacks guidance (watchlist, search, positions)
body:
Multiple components show empty states with no actionable guidance:

1. TopMovers.tsx:29 - Returns null if watchlist empty; user has no idea they need to add items
2. TopMovers.tsx:47-48 - "No data" (unclear why, user doesn't know if it's loading or empty)
3. SearchBar.tsx:60-66 - No results shown but no message like "No matches found for '{query}'"
4. PositionsList.tsx:67-75 - "No positions yet. Log a trade below." (good, but inconsistent tone vs others)
5. NewsPanel.tsx:14-15 - "News feed wires in Phase 2…" (placeholder, not an empty state)

Proposed: Standardize empty state copy with:
- What's missing (e.g., "Your watchlist is empty")
- Why (e.g., "Add stocks to track price changes")
- Action (e.g., "Search → Click heart icon")

Resilient apps: Empty state is not an error; guide user to fill it.

title: Network retry policy missing (fixed vs exponential backoff)
body:
React-query hooks define no explicit retry or backoff strategy:

- useQuote (line 39-45): no retry
- useStatements (line 51-56): no retry
- useFundamentals (line 74-79): no retry
- useSearch (line 72-78): no retry
- useHistorical (line 63-69): no retry

Default behavior: React-query retries 3 times with fixed delay (0ms). On Yahoo 429 (rate limit) or 503 (service unavailable), immediately retries with same cadence, hammering the already-overloaded endpoint.

Proposed:
1. Implement exponential backoff: retry after 500ms, 1500ms, 4500ms with jitter
2. Set per-hook: retry: (failureCount) => failureCount < 3, retryDelay: (count) => Math.min(1000 * 2 ** count, 10000) + Math.random() * 1000
3. Avoid querying user actions (search) without user gesture

Resilient apps: Respect rate limits, don't amplify outages.

title: PDF merge/split missing disk-full and permission error handling
body:
src/main/services/pdf/ops.ts implements error catching for read/write/parse failures, but missing edge cases:

1. mergePdfs (line 118-161): mkdir (line 113) can fail if:
   - Disk is full (ENOSPC)
   - Parent path is read-only (EACCES)
   - Symlink to restricted directory
   Error is not caught, crashes operation

2. splitPdf (line 169-197): writeFile (line 193) fails silently if outDir doesn't exist but mkdir isn't called (user provides invalid path)

3. Both functions: No check for available disk space before writing merged/split PDFs

4. No user-visible error message (errors thrown but IPC handler in electron main may not surface to renderer)

Proposed:
1. Wrap mkdir in try-catch, provide user feedback
2. Check available disk before merge (use os.freemem or fs.statfs)
3. Validate outDir writable before splitting
4. Return { success, error } instead of throwing, let renderer display error

Resilient apps: Graceful failure on disk/permission errors, not crashes.
evidence: src/main/services/pdf/ops.ts:112-115 (mkdir not wrapped), src/main/services/pdf/ops.ts:118-161 (no disk-full check)

title: Quote price null-safety: wrong default (breaks YoY calc)
body:
FinancialsCharts.tsx uses quote data with unsafe defaults:

1. Line 37-42: revenue = rows.income.map(r => r.revenue ?? null) ✓ correct
2. Line 87-92: yoy = (series) => series.map((v, i) => v == null || prev == null || prev === 0 ? null : ...) ✓ correct
3. BUT: Line 36 reads rows.income with no guard that it's non-empty
4. Line 267: fmtLargeNum(revenue[revenue.length - 1] ?? 0) - If all revenue is null, defaults to 0, implying "$0" revenue

StockHeader.tsx (line 61): quote?.price without fallback for undefined quote

TopMovers.tsx (line 19): q?.price ?? null (good), but line 59 uses fmtPrice(m.price) where m.price is null (displays "$—" or crashes)

Proposed:
1. Validate data.incomeAnnual.length > 0 before rendering charts
2. Never default numeric aggregates to 0 if data is sparse; use "—" or N/A
3. Guard quote?.price with explicit null-check before arithmetic

Severity: P3 - silently shows $0 revenue for zero-data companies, confusing users.

title: Unhandled promise rejection in SearchBar debounce + missing error state
body:
SearchBar.tsx (line 14-25) debounces search query but doesn't handle useSearch hook errors: 1. useSearch (useFinance.ts:72-78) defined with queryFn but no error handling in component 2. If Yahoo search endpoint returns 400/500, error state never displayed 3. Results show empty list (line 60: results.length > 0 renders dropdown) but user doesn't know it's an error vs empty 4. Debounce setTimeout (line 15) is cleanup correctly, but if fetch rejects, component ignores error Secondary: Line 53 uses results[0] without null-check if results array is empty (would work, but fragile). Proposed: 1. Add error state display: if (error) return
Search failed: {error.message}
2. Distinguish "no results" from "error" from "loading" 3. Provide retry button on error Resilient apps: Show error states clearly, not silence.
